Follow up. Here are some strategies for using the different mechanisms for follow up. First the phone. Thank you for coming by. You pick up your phone and you call somebody. Why would I want to contact somebody rather than email them or text them? Because I get to talk to them. I get to get a sense of communication. I get to hear what they say and what they don't say. I get to find out how hot they are or how cold they are or maybe they're lukewarm. I could say thanks for the opportunity. I really appreciate you coming by. I appreciate you hitting our website. I appreciate you calling us. Maybe they just sent you an email requesting information. Thank you for calling us. Here's the information. I need to call. I need to use the phone. Do not just rely on emails. If a customer visited your showroom you could literally pick up the phone and call them on their way home. I've done this before where somebody spent an hour, an hour and a half with me and either they left my company or I left their company because I was on my way out. I visited them or they came to me and they left me on the way home. I called them. Hey, let me ask you something. How'd you like our visit? I really appreciate the time you spent. You know, why didn't you leave without making the purchase? I've had people turn around and actually come back and say, you know what? We were just thinking we were going to come back, glad you called and did the deal. It was just to follow what call. It was literally picking up a phone and taking a shot. Another way to call people is just to ask them, hey I need your help. I need some feedback. It's like a survey over the phone. Give me some feedback. You know, the reason I'm calling is to get some feedback from you about how your visit was here today. If you're uncomfortable making that call, you could always have somebody else do the call for you as a manager. Look, the phone is your best use of time. I mean, it is the best use of time short of texting somebody. It gets you to talk to them. I get to communicate again. I establish that line of communication with them and be sure that you listen, don't just talk. I'm trying to extract data in this call. Listen also for what they don't say as much as what they do say. Your phone call is direct communication. The sooner you do it, the better you are. Pay attention to what they're saying. Have a good attitude. Don't make them feel like they're obligated. Don't go for the close in this call, but you could. I'll be explaining that in future strategies. Make the call. Make it as quick as possible. Be friendly. Have a positive attitude. Remember, you're on a track here. Okay. It's five to 12 contacts. Maybe more than that. Who knows where they're going to drop. Pay attention. They could say, we're ready to do it now. It may be only the second call. Follow up, follow up, follow up. The phone is just one mechanism.
